content.problemStatement:
You are given a positive integer KKK and an array AAA consisting of NNN non-negative integers. Find the lexicographically smallest sequence BBB that satisfies the following conditions


We can obtain BBB by rearranging the elements in AAA.
The length of longest non-decreasing subsequence of BBB is equal to KKK.
If no such sequence exists, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

First line of the input contains TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
First line of each test case contains two space separated integers NNN and KKK.
Second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers describing the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the array BBB in a single line with a space between two consecutive elements. And output −1-1−1 if no such array BBB exists.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 200200200.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 666 arrays that are rearrangements of the array [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3].

[1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 333.
[1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.
[2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.
[2,3,1][2, 3, 1][2,3,1] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.
[3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.
[3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 111.

Test case 1:
Observe from the list above that [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] is the only rearrangement of [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3] with the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 111. And hence [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1] is the lexicographically smallest that satisfies the given conditions.
Test case 2:
Observe from the above list the [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] is the lexicographically smallest among all those rearrangements with the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222. And hence [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] is the lexicographically smallest that satisfies the given conditions.
Test case 3:
Observe from the list above that [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] is the only rearrangement of [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3] with the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 333. And hence [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] is the lexicographically smallest that satisfies the given conditions.
Test case 4:
There are only 333 possible ways to rearrange [2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3].

[2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 333.
[2,3,2][2, 3, 2][2,3,2] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.
[3,2,2][3, 2, 2][3,2,2] with length of longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 222.

So there does not exist any rearrangement of [2,2,3][2, 2, 3][2,2,3] with the length of the longest non-decreasing subsequence equal to 111.
